notes.note: The suburban estate of Furius Crassipes, Cicero's son-in-law of 56 BC; just outside the city-line and a frequent dinner venue. A dinner there counted, technically, as ``at an inn'' rather than ``at home''.

notes.anchor: playing a trick on the senate's decree
notes.note: Probably a reference to the recurrent senatorial sumptuary regulation governing dinners at home. By dining at Crassipes's suburban gardens (technically extra-urban) Cicero takes himself outside the decree's reach.
The first day of the month; here, the Kalends of June 55 BC. Cicero plans to dine on the Kalends and again on the day after.
The thirteenth of the month, or the fifteenth in March, May, July, and October; here the Ides of May 55 BC, when Cicero will be at Larinum for an auction.
